channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A father and son are both indicted on murder charges in a mass school shooting in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia grand jury indicted both a father and son on murder charges Thursday in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School in Winder.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Liam Payne died from 'multiple traumas,' 'internal and external bleeding,' autopsy reveals
item.description: Officials have released the cause of death of Liam Payne, the former member of the British boy band One Direction. According to Argentina's National Criminal and Correctional Prosecutor's Office No.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: The Latest: Harris keeps a focus on 'blue wall' states in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Uphill battles that put abortion rights on ballots are unlikely to end even if the measures pass
item.description: Voters in nine states are deciding next month whether to add the right to abortion to their constitutions, but the measures are unlikely to dramatically change access — at least not immediately.
item.dc:creator: GEOFF MULVIHILL, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: New renderings show how A's ballpark would transform Las Vegas Strip
item.description: Extensive details have been revealed about the proposed $1.5 billion "multi-purpose, enclosed ballpark" on the Las Vegas Strip that could become home to the Las Vegas Athletics MLB franchise in time for the 2028 baseball season.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mitzi Gaynor, ‘South Pacific’ star, dies at 93
item.description: Mitzi Gaynor, the effervescent dancer and actor who starred as Nellie Forbush in the 1958 film "South Pacific" and appeared in other musicals with Bing Crosby, Frank Sinatra and Gene Kelly, has died. She was 93.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Liam Payne, former One Direction member, dies at 31 in Argentina hotel fall
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Former One Direction singer Liam Payne, 31, whose chart-topping British boy band generated a global following of swooning fans, was found dead Wednesday after falling from a hotel balcony in Buenos Aires, local officials said.
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE and KAITLYN HUAMANI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Florida digs out of mountains of sand swept in by back-to-back hurricanes
item.description: BRADENTON BEACH, Fla. (AP) — When a hurricane sets its sights on Florida, storm-weary residents may think of catastrophic wind, hammering rain and dangerous storm surge. Mounds of sand swallowing their homes? Not so much.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Electoral battleground North Carolina starts early in-person voting while recovering from Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (AP) — Early in-person voting began statewide Thursday in the presidential battleground of North Carolina, including in mountainous areas where thousands of potential voters still lack power and clean running water after Hurricane Helene's epic flooding.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 3 dead, several injured after bridge collapses in Mississippi
item.description: Three people have died and several others were injured after a bridge collapsed in Mississippi on Wednesday, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Cameron Smith, Fox 5

item.title: The Biden administration has now canceled loans for more than 1 million public workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A student loan cancellation program for public workers has granted relief to more than 1 million Americans — up from just 7,000 who were approved before it was updated by the Biden administration two years ago.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Asian American evangelicals' theology is conservative. But that doesn't mean they vote that way
item.description: The Rev. Wayne Lee leads an English-speaking church of second- and third-generation Chinese Americans in the heart of Philadelphia’s Chinatown.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Oklahoma investigation finds leaders ‘grossly negligent’ in management of pandemic relief funds
item.description: A newly unsealed multicounty grand jury report in Oklahoma found mismanagement of millions of dollars in the Governor's Emergency Education Relief Fund (GEER).
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A historically Black community grapples with lasting impacts after Baltimore bridge collapse
item.description: In the aftermath of the deadly Baltimore bridge collapse, residents of a historically Black community northeast of the city are facing an uncertain future.
item.dc:creator: Lea Skene, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Judge invalidates election rules passed by Trump-backed board in Georgia
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A Georgia judge has declared that seven new election rules recently passed by the State Election Board are “illegal, unconstitutional and void.” Fulton County Superior Court Judge Thomas Cox issued the order Wednesday after holding a hearing on challenges to the rules.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Harris holds 2-point edge in tight North Carolina race: Poll
item.description: Vice President Harris holds a 2-point lead over former President Trump among likely voters in North Carolina, according to a poll released Wednesday. The Quinnipiac University poll found the vice president garnering the support of 49 percent of likely voters to the former president’s 47 percent.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Pro-Israel professor at Columbia barred from campus after harassing and intimidating other employees
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — An outspoken pro-Israel professor at Columbia University has been temporarily barred from campus, a university spokesperson said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIP MARCELO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man arrested near Trump Coachella rally with gun sues for defamation
item.description: The man arrested near former President Trump’s Coachella, Calif., rally over the weekend sued the Riverside County sheriff for defamation and other claims, saying there were “preposterous allegations” made about the incident possibly being an assassination attempt.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-Las Vegas-area Democratic politician gets at least 28 years in prison for killing reporter
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A former Las Vegas-area Democratic elected official was sentenced Wednesday to serve at least 28 years in Nevada state prison for killing an investigative journalist who wrote articles critical of his conduct in office two years ago and exposed an intimate relationship with a female
item.dc:creator: KEN RITTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dog spotted on top of Great Pyramid of Giza
item.description: Talk about a long walk. A paraglider getting an aerial view of the Great Pyramids of Giza in Egypt on Monday was surprised to see a dog atop the iconic giant pyramid.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Killer's escape from Pennsylvania jail costs him his right to appeal life sentence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A convicted killer’s escape from a Pennsylvania jail last year has cost him his right to appeal his original life sentence, a state court ruled Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Former Las Vegas-area official sentenced in murder of journalist
item.description: The former public administrator was accused of killing Jeff German after the reporter wrote articles critical of his conduct in office.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, ABC 4

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Protesters demand Kellogg remove artificial colors from Froot Loops and other cereals
item.description: Dozens of people have rallied outside the Michigan headquarters of WK Kellogg Co. to demand that the company remove artificial dyes from their cereals in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Menendez brothers' family to push for their release as prosecutors review 1989 case
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The extended family of Erik and Lyle Menendez will advocate for the brothers' release from prison following the 1989 killings of their parents in Beverly Hills.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Suter, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Former Vegas Democrat politician convicted of killing reporter is sentenced to at least 28 years
item.description: Robert Telles, a former Las Vegas-area Democratic politician convicted of killing a journalist, faces at least 28 years in prison before he becomes eligible for parole.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Injured Alex Cobb dropped from Guardians' ALCS roster, replaced by Ben Lively
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Pitcher Alex Cobb was dropped from Cleveland's American League Championship Series roster on Tuesday after he got hurt yet again in the opener, leaving the Guardians in search of a Game 5 starter against the New York Yankees. “It’s been a challenging year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Voters in California and Nevada consider ban on forced labor aimed at protecting prisoners
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California and Nevada voters will decide in November whether to ban forced prison labor by removing language from their state constitutions rooted in the legacy of chattel slavery.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN and RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump rally turns into awkward dance party
item.description: What began as a campaign town hall in the Philadelphia suburbs quickly turned into an impromptu dance party Monday night after Donald Trump was twice interrupted by medical emergencies in the audience.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: Convicted killer Susan Smith charged after speaking with filmmaker
item.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WSPA) - Convicted killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a disciplinary charge weeks before her parole hearing. Smith, of Union County, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of her children in Oct. 1994. The SC Department of Corrections confirmed with 7NEWS that on Aug.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: 6 people accuse Diddy of sexual assault in new lawsuits, including man who was 16 at the time
item.description: Twice denied bail, the Bad Boy Records founder remains locked up a Brooklyn federal jail while awaiting trial in May.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Which are the most popular Halloween candies in Utah?
item.description: Are you hoping the neighborhood mischief-makers won’t “T.P.” your house on Halloween? Then maybe you should hand out the candies they actually like.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4

item.title: FDA shakes up weight loss market as Mounjaro shortage ends
item.description: Federal regulators shook up the booming weight loss drug marketplace when they announced Mounjaro and Zepbound to be no longer in shortage after nearly two years.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Members of the Kennedy family gather for funeral of Ethel Kennedy
item.description: CENTERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — Members of the Kennedy family gathered Monday for the funeral of Ethel Kennedy, the wife of the late Sen. Robert F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: List: Here are the nearly 500 Big Lots stores that have or will be closed this year
item.description: Since filing for bankruptcy protection earlier this year, Big Lots has slated nearly 500 stores for closure - some of which have already closed their doors for good.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Disturbing video shows road rage suspect fatally shooting man in Los Angeles
item.description: Video obtained exclusively by KTLA shows the terrifying moment a road rage suspect opened fire on the driver of another car on the 5 Freeway in Los Angeles, fatally wounding him and seriously injuring a passenger.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, Fox 5
